content.full_text:
June 14, 1976

Mr. Joseph E. Sheedy  
Member, Board of Supervisors  
County of Sacramento  
827 Seventh Street  
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Sheedy:

Thank you for your letter of June 9, 1976 requesting clarification of your filing obligations under the Political Reform Act.

In response to your inquiry concerning the receipt of funds as a result of a fund raiser, the proceeds of which are to be spent to defray the costs of attending the Democratic Convention in New York, these funds need not be listed on your campaign statement as a contribution to your campaign unless the money is deposited in your campaign account or in any manner commingled with political funds earmarked to be used on behalf of your candidacy.

You will, however, be required to list receipt of the funds on your Statement of Economic Interest as a gift.

If you desire additional information, please do not hesitate to let me know.

Very truly yours,

REGINA SICILIANO-KUTCHINS  
Technical Assistance Program Coordinator

RSK: mak

bcc: Jeanne

June 9, 1976

Ms. Regina Kutchins  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
1100 "K" Street  
Sacramento, CA 95814  

Dear Ms. Kutchins:

A few of my friends are organizing a fund-raiser to defer the costs of attending the Democratic Convention in New York.

Therefore, I would like to know if the funds I raise would be considered campaign contributions and reportable under the Fair Political Practices Act. In addition, must these funds be reported as a gift under the financial disclosure provisions of the Act?

Sincerely,

TED SHEEDY
